Land Rover Defender D250 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender D250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Defender D250 (AJ20D6) engine typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,700 depending on location and workshop rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender D250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is a 3.0-litre six-cylinder Ingenium diesel introduced with the L663 Defender in 2020. It's generally considered more robust than the 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium diesels, though it shares the rear-mounted timing chain design. A 2026 recall affected 2024–2027 models for a FEAD belt idler pulley issue — but this is a free fix, not a fundamental engine flaw.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender D250?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) FEAD belt idler pulley failure on 2024–2027 models (free recall fix), (2) timing chain stretch — shared with the wider Ingenium diesel family, and (3) oil dilution from DPF regeneration on vehicles used predominantly for short urban trips.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender D250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £40,000+ Defender D250, a £4,500 recon is around 11% of the car's value — worth it. The L663 Defender holds its value extremely well, making engine replacement a viable option on almost all examples.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender D250 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D250 uses the AJ20D6 (also known as DT306) — a 3.0-litre six-cylinder diesel. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ20D6 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£4,500–£9,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender D250 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current L663-generation AJ20D6, the only engine used in the Defender D250.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender D250 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare Defender D250 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: FEAD Belt Idler Pulley Failure (2024–2027 models)
problems.symptoms: Engine oil leak, oil pressure warning light, potential oil temperature sensor damage
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affects 2024–2027 model year vehicles
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: This is a genuine safety recall affecting certain AJ20-D6 engines where the FEAD belt idler pulley can over-rotate after high-torsion input and damage the oil pressure sensor. The fix is free at any JLR dealership. Before buying any 2024–2027 Defender D250, confirm the recall work has been completed — it's a straightforward check that could save you from an oil leak and potential roadside hazard.

failureLink.label: Read the full FEAD belt failure guide → /failures/fead-belt-idler-pulley/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (Ingenium Diesel Family)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle, metallic ticking from engine, engine warning light, reduced performance
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles (more common on earlier Ingenium diesels)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you hear rattle

problems.recommendation: The AJ20D6 shares the Ingenium diesel family's rear-mounted timing chain design. While the six-cylinder unit is generally more robust than the 2.0-litre four-cylinder, chain stretch remains a known pattern across the Ingenium diesel range. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected — a £1,200–£2,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £4,500+ replacement that follows a snapped chain.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Oil Dilution (DPF Regeneration)
problems.symptoms: Rising oil level on dipstick, diesel smell in oil, DPF warning light, reduced fuel economy
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — short journeys accelerate the issue
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: Like all modern diesel engines with DPF systems, the AJ20D6 can suffer from oil dilution if the car is used predominantly for short urban trips. The engine's mild-hybrid system helps, but it doesn't eliminate the need for regular motorway running to complete passive DPF regenerations. If you're buying a Defender D250 primarily for city use, factor in occasional longer runs or consider the petrol P400 instead.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil dilution guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender D250 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Regular oil changes and DPF regeneration cycles
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall engine health

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender D250? Check These First
buyIf: * 2024–2027 model with confirmed FEAD recall completion
* Full service history with 10,000–12,000-mile oil intervals
* Evidence of regular motorway use (DPF health)
* Low-mileage example with documented chain inspection history
avoidIf: * 2024–2027 model with no proof of FEAD recall work
* Cold-start rattle and no timing chain history
* Poor service history — the Ingenium family is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Defender D250's AJ20D6 is a modern, sophisticated six-cylinder diesel that represents a meaningful step up from the four-cylinder Ingenium units in terms of refinement and, by most accounts, reliability. The FEAD pulley recall is the single biggest known issue — but it's a free fix, not a design flaw. Buy on documented history, with recall confirmation, and you're looking at one of the more robust diesel options in the L663 range.

cta.label: → Compare Defender D250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender D250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine (AJ20D6) and confirmed oil-interval discipline (10k–12k miles)
* Recall Check — Verify FEAD belt idler pulley recall completion on 2024–2027 models
* Smoke Check — Excessive white smoke on startup can indicate injector or DPF issues
* Oil Level — Check for rising oil level (oil dilution from DPF regeneration)
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* DPF Regeneration History — Short journeys accelerate wear on all modern diesels
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
